note: Get returns canonical keys: numberformat (not `format`), alignment.{horizontal,vertical,wrapText}, font.{bold,italic,name,size,color}. Add/Set accept both font.* and short forms (bold, italic, font, size). The bare `color` key is rejected on cells (ambiguous with `fill`) — use `font.color`. Parent path controls placement: `/Sheet1` appends to the next empty cell; `/Sheet1/A2` targets a specific cell.

value.description: literal cell value — string, number, or date. Numeric strings stored as numbers unless cell has text format (@) or explicit type=string. Readback in DocumentNode.Text.

numberformat.description: Excel format code — covers number, date, percentage, currency, and text (@). "@" forces String storage on subsequent values.

font.bold.description: bold font weight on the cell.

font.name.description: font family name. Aliases: font, fontname.

font.color.description: font color on the cell. Note: the bare 'color' alias is intentionally rejected on cells due to ambiguity with 'fill' (background) — use 'font.color' explicitly.

fill.description: cell background fill. Solid color (hex / named / rgb(...)) or a linear gradient as 'COLOR1-COLOR2[-ANGLE]' / 'gradient;COLOR1;COLOR2[;ANGLE]'. Scheme color names (accent1..) and 'none' are not accepted on input — readback may surface them when a cell already carries them.
fill.readback: #RRGGBB uppercase, 'gradient;#START;#END;ANGLE' for gradients, scheme color name (e.g. accent1) when set externally

strike.description: single strikethrough on the cell text.

underline.description: underline style on the cell text.

locked.description: cell protection: lock the cell against edits when the sheet is protected. Default Excel behavior is locked=true. Add/Set only — readback is exposed under 'protection.locked'.

protection.locked.description: cell protection lock state. Get-only readback (dotted form). For Add/Set use the flat `locked` key.

protection.hidden.description: hide formula in protected sheet. Get-only readback.

numFmtId.description: raw OOXML number format id (supplementary; prefer `numberformat`). Emitted only when numFmtId > 0.

phonetic.description: phonetic guide text from SST PhoneticRun. Emitted only when present.

quotePrefix.description: leading apostrophe quote-prefix flag. Emitted only when set.

alignment.wrapText.description: wrap text within the cell. Aliases: wrap, wrapText.

alignment.readingOrder.description: cell text reading direction (OOXML 0=context, 1=ltr, 2=rtl). Use 'rtl' for Arabic / Hebrew, 'ltr' to force left-to-right, 'context' (default) to derive from content.

merge.description: merge range applied post-cell-creation (parity with `set`). Accepts a single A1 range (A1:C3) or comma-separated ranges (A1:B1,A2:B2). Use merge=false to clear an existing merge anchored at this cell (aliases: unmerge, none, empty).

ref.description: target A1 cell reference (alternative to encoding the address in the path tail).

link.description: hyperlink target attached to the cell. Accepts external URL (https://, http://, mailto:, file://, onenote:, tel:) or internal anchor (#Sheet!Cell, #NamedRange). Use link=none on Set to remove. Parity with Set.
link.readback: URL string or internal anchor as stored

tooltip.description: ScreenTip text shown on hover for an existing hyperlink. Pair with link= during Add, or apply to a cell that already has a hyperlink during Set.

type.description: force a cell type. Normally inferred from value/formula. Add/Set accept the listed values only; SST-backed shared strings and inline strings are not creatable via Add (use plain string instead). Get may still surface 'SharedString' or 'InlineString' when reading cells written by Excel or other tools.

runs.description: rich-text runs as JSON array (e.g. '[{"text":"Hello","bold":true}]'). Used when type=richtext.

clear.description: clear the cell value/formula before applying new content.

shift.description: Cell-level Insert/Delete shift (Excel UI parity). On `add --type cell`: 'right' pushes existing cells in the same row right by one to make room; 'down' pushes existing cells in the same column down by one. On `remove` (passed via the top-level `--shift` flag, not `--prop`): 'left' shifts cells in the same row left into the gap; 'up' shifts cells in the same column up. Scope cap: only cellRefs within the affected row (left/right) or column (up/down) are rewritten — formulas, mergeCells, and CF/DV/hyperlink/table refs that span the affected band are NOT adjusted. For full-band shift with all metadata adjusted, use add/remove --type row or --type col.

arrayformula.description: dynamic-array formula spilled into ref range (without leading '=').

cachedValue.description: raw <x:v> cached display value as stored in the file. Surfaces only on Get/Query for formula cells; absent on plain-value cells. Mirrors what the last writer (Excel/LibreOffice) computed — may be stale.
cachedValue.readback: raw cached display value for formula cells; absent on plain-value cells

computedValue.description: OfficeCli evaluator's prediction of the formula result. Surfaces only on Get/Query for formula cells when the evaluator could compute a value. Compare against cachedValue to detect stale caches (see view issues subtype formula_cache_stale).
computedValue.readback: evaluator-predicted value for formula cells; absent when the evaluator could not compute

evaluated.description: cross-handler protocol flag — true when cachedValue is present OR the evaluator produced computedValue; false when the formula has neither (view text substitutes #OCLI_NOTEVAL!). Read this via get --json instead of pattern-matching the sentinel, since literal user content may collide. Subtype: formula_not_evaluated.
evaluated.readback: true|false (true ⇒ either cachedValue or computedValue is available)

alignment.indent.description: cell alignment indent units (CT_CellAlignment @indent). Use the flat `indent` key on Add/Set.

alignment.textRotation.description: cell alignment text rotation in degrees (CT_CellAlignment @textRotation).

font.subscript.description: font vertical-alignment subscript flag (legacy alias of cell-level `subscript`).

font.superscript.description: font vertical-alignment superscript flag (legacy alias of cell-level `superscript`).

border.diagonal.description: diagonal border line style (CT_Border/diagonal @style — thin, medium, thick, dashed, etc.).

border.diagonalDown.description: true when the cell shows a top-left → bottom-right diagonal border.

border.diagonalUp.description: true when the cell shows a bottom-left → top-right diagonal border.

arrayref.description: array-formula spill range (CellFormula @ref). Surfaces on the master cell of an array formula.

mergeAnchor.description: true when this cell is the top-left anchor of a merged range. Empty merged-region cells receive mergeAnchor=false; the anchor receives true.

empty.description: true when the cell has neither display text nor a formula. Useful for distinguishing styled-but-empty cells from data cells.

richtext.description: true when the cell stores rich-text runs (multi-format text). Surfaces alongside `runs` in Get output.

subscript.description: cell-level run subscript flag (when cell is rich text with a single run).

superscript.description: cell-level run superscript flag (when cell is rich text with a single run).
